entries.varden_fit: VERY HIGH - Direct infrastructure play. Varden has 15+ EHR integrations - Leyr's unified API
VERY HIGH - Direct infrastructure play. Varden could use this to accelerate EHR integrations globally.
HIGH - Infrastructure for data access. Could enable Varden to query external data sources.
MEDIUM - Enterprise integration platform. Different scale than Varden but demonstrates market need.
LOW - China-specific, regulatory complexity. But demonstrates global need for integration infrastructure.
MEDIUM - Similar infrastructure thesis. Latin America is underrepresented market for Varden expansion.
MEDIUM - Data capture infrastructure. Could integrate with Varden's intake and documentation flows.
LOW - Regional EHR vendor. But shows need for localized integration solutions.
MEDIUM - Enterprise competitor at different scale. Validates infrastructure thesis.
HIGH - Direct infrastructure play. Similar model to what Varden could offer developers.
HIGH - FHIR-native infrastructure. Aligned with modern healthcare data standards.

notes: EXPANDED - Now 12 companies solving healthcare data integration. Includes global infrastructure plays (Redox, Health Gorilla), regional solutions (Orion Health APAC, Praxis LatAm, MEDISPA Middle East), and developer-first platforms (Particle Health, 1upHealth). Key infrastructure thesis validated across markets.
